skill.name: sending-emails
skill.description: Use when integrating, configuring, or troubleshooting Mailtrap live email sending (Email API or SMTP). Use when wiring outbound mail from an application or choosing how to send.
skill.summary: Integrate Mailtrap transactional and bulk email sending via Email API or SMTP, with guidance on choosing the right approach, endpoints, and authentication.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: The skill is a single markdown documentation file describing Mailtrap's email sending API and SMTP integration. All 56 'external_commands' findings are false positives triggered by markdown backticks used for inline code and bash code blocks containing legitimate curl examples. All 35 'network' findings are false positives — the hardcoded URLs are Mailtrap's official API endpoints (send.api.mailtrap.io, bulk.api.mailtrap.io) documented for user reference. No executable code exists in the file; the skill cannot execute commands or make network requests on its own. Risk is low due to the nature of the content (documentation only) referencing email-sending endpoints.

low_findings.title: Markdown code formatting triggers command-execution false positives
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 56 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings. All are false positives: the file is a markdown documentation file. The backticks are markdown syntax for inline code and fenced code blocks containing legitimate curl examples. There is no Ruby or shell code that gets executed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API endpoints in reference documentation
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged 35 'Hardcoded URL' findings. All are false positives: the URLs are Mailtrap's official, publicly documented API endpoints (send.api.mailtrap.io, bulk.api.mailtrap.io, live.smtp.mailtrap.io) intentionally listed as reference for users integrating with the service. No data exfiltration risk.

low_findings.title: Email sending capability (intended purpose)
low_findings.description: The skill's stated purpose is guiding users to send emails via Mailtrap. The 'Email sending capability' flag is expected and is the core function of this skill, not a security concern. The skill itself is documentation; it does not send emails directly.

content.user_title: Send emails reliably with Mailtrap API and SMTP
content.value_statement: Setting up outbound email for an application often means choosing between APIs, SDKs, and SMTP while avoiding common mistakes. This skill provides clear guidance for integrating Mailtrap transactional and bulk email sending, so your team can ship email features quickly and correctly.
actual_capabilities: Guide integration of Mailtrap Email API (REST) for transactional and bulk streams
Explain SMTP configuration including host, port, username, and authentication
Recommend the preferred integration order: platform plugin, SDK, HTTP API, then SMTP
Provide curl examples for single, batch, and template-based email sends
Clarify the difference between batch (many messages per request) and bulk (promotional stream)
Reference official SDK repositories for Node.js, Python, PHP, Ruby, Java, and .NET
limitations: Does not cover Sandbox email testing (handled by a separate testing-with-sandbox skill)
Does not provide exhaustive API reference; links to official docs for full schemas
Does not replace Campaigns UI setup for promotional sends to Mailtrap contacts
Does not include deliverability deep-dives or webhook configuration guidance

use_cases.title: Adding transactional email to a web application
use_cases.description: A developer needs to send password resets, receipts, and notifications from a new app. This skill guides them to pick the right Mailtrap stream and integrate using SDK or HTTP API.

use_cases.title: Migrating from sandbox to live email sending
use_cases.description: A team finishes testing and needs to switch from Mailtrap Sandbox to live sending. This skill explains the endpoint changes, token scopes, and common migration mistakes.

use_cases.title: Sending promotional email at volume
use_cases.description: A marketing team wants to send newsletters and promotions. This skill helps them choose the Bulk stream, configure the correct base URL, and understand batch sending limits.

prompt_templates.title: Basic transactional email setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Mailtrap transactional email sending for my Node.js app. I need to send password reset emails. Show me the SDK install steps and a minimal send example.

prompt_templates.title: Choosing between API and SMTP
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building a Python app and unsure whether to use the Mailtrap Email API or SMTP. Walk me through the decision factors and show example code for both approaches.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk promotional campaign setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to send a weekly newsletter to 5,000 subscribers using Mailtrap. Should I use the Bulk stream or Campaigns? Explain the tradeoffs and show me a curl example for the bulk API.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up marketing email at scale

prompt_templates.title: Batch sending multiple different messages
prompt_templates.prompt: My app generates many different notification emails at once. Show me how to use the Mailtrap /api/batch endpoint to send up to 500 different messages in a single request, with a JSON payload example.

output_examples.input: Explain how to send a single transactional email with the Mailtrap HTTP API using curl.
output: Use a POST request to the transactional send endpoint with a Bearer token.
Include from, to, subject, and text or html fields in the JSON body.
Example: curl -X POST https://send.api.mailtrap.io/api/send with Authorization header and JSON payload containing the email details.

output_examples.input: What is the difference between batch and bulk in Mailtrap?
output: Batch means submitting many messages in a single API request (up to 500).
Bulk refers to the promotional email stream and its dedicated base URL (bulk.api.mailtrap.io).
You can use batch on both transactional and bulk streams.

output_examples.input: What authentication does Mailtrap SMTP require?
output: Username: api. Password: your Mailtrap API token.

best_practices: Choose the integration approach in order: platform plugin, official SDK, HTTP API, then SMTP only when necessary.
Read the SDK README before writing code to confirm current method signatures and constructor options.
Use the correct base URL for each stream: send.api.mailtrap.io for transactional, bulk.api.mailtrap.io for promotional.
Complete Sending Domains setup and compliance verification before sending live email.
anti_patterns: Sending promotional content through the transactional host or token.
Using sandbox SMTP credentials for live production sending.
Guessing SDK API signatures from memory instead of reading current documentation.
Choosing SMTP as the first option for a new application when an SDK or HTTP API is available.

faq.question: What is the difference between transactional and bulk streams?
faq.answer: Transactional is for app-generated, non-promotional email like receipts and notifications. Bulk is the promotional or marketing stream for higher-volume sends to contacts you manage.

faq.question: Can I use the same API token for both transactional and bulk?
faq.answer: Yes, one Mailtrap API token can work on both streams as long as its scope covers the stream you are sending to.

faq.question: What are the Mailtrap sending rate limits?
faq.answer: The sending API allows 150 requests per token per 10 seconds. Use backoff when you receive a 429 response.

faq.question: Is batch the same as bulk?
faq.answer: No. Batch means sending many different messages in one API request (up to 500). Bulk is a stream type for promotional email. You can apply batch to either stream.

faq.question: Do I need to verify my domain before sending?
faq.answer: Yes. Complete the Sending Domains setup and compliance checks in Mailtrap before sending live email from your domain.

faq.question: When should I use SMTP instead of the API?
faq.answer: Use SMTP only when your host or platform only supports SMTP, or when integrating with a legacy stack. Prefer the SDK or HTTP API for new applications.
